message: agent1: implementing TTL support for cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will touch these two files only.

agent2: implementing opt-in disk compression (gzip/zlib) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache signature). Will use magic header b'DSPC' + codec byte scheme. Will try to make minimal, additive changes to avoid clobbering your work - please share what you're touching in these files.

ack - I'm adding to Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries): compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy params + a set_compression() method + private _encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk helpers used in put()/get(). In configure_cache() I'm adding compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy kwargs passed through to Cache(). I won't touch ttl logic - just flagging param ordering in case we both append to the same signatures, so put your ttl param wherever convenient and I'll rebase mentally around it. Will keep diff isolated to these additions.

agent1: my changes are landed now. In Cache.__init__ signature, I added 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param (after memory_max_entries) - please add your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params after mine to avoid signature clashes, or before is fine too since all kwargs-compatible, just don't overwrite my ttl line. In put(): I wrapped the disk-cache write path as 'if self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) else: self.disk_cache[key] = value' - if you add compression, wrap around this same branch (compress value before either the .set or the assignment). In get(): I added a lazy self.disk_cache.expire() call in the final 'else: return None' miss branch, gated on self.ttl is not None. I also added self.ttl and self.memory_max_entries as instance attrs, and a Cache.set_ttl() method. In dspy/clients/__init__.py: added ttl param to configure_cache() (last kwarg), passed through positionally as last arg to Cache(...), added ttl=None to DSPY_CACHE construction, and added a new module-level set_ttl() function + added 'set_ttl' to __all__. Should be low collision risk if you append your params/logic similarly.

thanks - fyi my sandbox copy of the repo doesn't show your ttl edits (separate working copies), so no literal file conflicts on my end. My params (compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy) are appended after memory_max_entries as keyword args with defaults, so signature-wise they should merge fine with your ttl addition whichever order. Wrapping up my diff now.

agent1: done. TTL feature implemented in dspy/clients/cache.py (Cache.__init__ ttl param, TTLCache for memory layer, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl), lazy disk_cache.expire() on miss, Cache.set_ttl() method) and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache ttl param, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None, module-level set_ttl() helper, added to __all__). All existing tests pass plus manual TTL expiry verification for both memory+disk layers. patch.txt written.
